analysis_summary: The proposed method produced 6 finding(s) from 6 analysis obligation(s).

findings.summary: ROM2 secure_reg is writable with no internal protection; depends entirely on interconnect gating which is unverified
findings.vulnerability_category: write / rom2 secure_reg (boot ROM memory array) / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: AXI master (any bus master with a connection to ROM2) Operation: write Object: rom2 secure_reg (boot ROM memory array) Expected guard: Write-once lock bit or write-protection signal inside rom2, OR verified interconnect gating Observed behavior: rom2 unconditionally accepts writes when req_i && we_i; no internal write-protection mechanism exists Path: Master → AXI write channel → axi_node_intf_wrap → axi_node (unseen) → ariane_peripherals (unseen, .rom2_fuse) → rom2 we_i/wdata_i → secure_reg update Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Keys, JTAG secrets, and access-control configuration can be overwritten by any master that bypasses or is granted interconnect access
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Interconnect enforcement by connectivity_map_o not verified; no internal write-protection in rom2

findings.summary: Debug master shares processor's privilege level, allowing potentially unintended accesses
findings.vulnerability_category: read / write / rom2 (and all other protected slaves) / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: debug master (i_axi_master_dm) Operation: read / write Object: rom2 (and all other protected slaves) Expected guard: Debug master subject to its own privilege-level check or forced to a restricted privilege; separate per-initiator priv_lvl Observed behavior: Single priv_lvl_i shared across all initiators (both ariane and debug); debug master inherits processor's privilege for access-control decisions Path: JTAG/DMI → dmi_jtag → debug_req → dm_top → i_axi_master_dm → slave[1] → axi_node_intf_wrap → connectivity_mapping (using processor's priv_lvl) Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Debug master can access ROM2 and other restricted peripherals if the processor is in a high privilege mode, bypassing any intended debug-specific restrictions
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: No per-initiator priv_lvl; authentication (jtag_key) not used to modify privilege level

findings.summary: Connectivity map is computed but its gating effect cannot be verified from available RTL
findings.vulnerability_category: gate AXI requests (both read and write) / all slaves, especially ROM2 (j=9) / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: connectivity_map_o signal in axi_node_intf_wrap Operation: gate AXI requests (both read and write) Object: all slaves, especially ROM2 (j=9) Expected guard: connectivity_map_o[i][j]=0 must block AW/AR/W channel transactions to target j from initiator i Observed behavior: connectivity_map_o computed and passed to axi_node module; no gating logic visible in the inspected RTL Path: connectivity_map_o → s_connectivity_map → cfg_connectivity_map_i → axi_node (unseen) → ??? Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: If axi_node does not enforce the connectivity map, the entire access-control matrix is ineffective
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: The axi_node module implementation (where gating would occur) is not in the visible file set; cannot confirm enforcement

findings.summary: Security of access_ctrl_reg cannot be assessed without its driving module
findings.vulnerability_category: write (configuration change) / access_ctrl_reg content / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: access_ctrl_reg (CSR/MMIO register storing access control matrix) Operation: write (configuration change) Object: access_ctrl_reg content Expected guard: access_ctrl_reg should be writable only by highest privilege level and locked after secure boot, or be hard-wired Observed behavior: access_ctrl_reg is declared as logic [1:0][47:0], sourced from ariane_peripherals module (unseen); no write-protection logic visible Path: ariane_peripherals (unseen) → access_ctrl_reg → slicing to access_ctrl → connectivity_map_o Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: Attacker could modify access permissions to grant unauthorized ROM2 writes
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: The ariane_peripherals module (which drives access_ctrl_reg) is not in the file set; cannot determine write protection

findings.summary: Read access restriction depends entirely on unverified interconnect gating
findings.vulnerability_category: read / rom2 secure_reg contents / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: any AXI master Operation: read Object: rom2 secure_reg contents Expected guard: Read access to ROM2 restricted via connectivity_map_o gating on the AR channel Observed behavior: rom2 provides combinational read data via rdata_o (raddr_q-based); no internal access control; connectivity_map_o gating not verified for reads Path: Master → AR channel → axi_node_intf_wrap → (connectivity_map_o check?) → ariane_peripherals → rom2 rdata_o output Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: If connectivity_map_o does not gate reads, sensitive keys and access-control defaults can be read by any master
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: Connectivity_map enforcement for AR channel not visible; same uncertainty as OB03

findings.summary: Test enable is disabled at top level, but its effect inside the unseen axi_node is unknown
findings.vulnerability_category: override access controls / AXI slave access gating logic / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: test_en_i signal Operation: override access controls Object: AXI slave access gating logic Expected guard: test_en_i must not disable access-control gating; tied low in production Observed behavior: test_en_i is tied low in testharness and only forwarded to axi_node module; no internal usage in visible wrap Path: test_en = 1'b0 → i_axi_xbar.test_en_i → axi_node.test_en_i (unseen) Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: If axi_node uses test_en_i to bypass connectivity_map, debug/test mode could circumvent protections
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: The axi_node module (where test_en_i is consumed) is not available; cannot verify that test_en does not bypass access controls

global_uncertainty: No closure-level missing evidence was recorded beyond the visible source scope limitations.
